The records share route_id BM5, route_type_normalized express, service_variant express, and Brooklyn context; the evidence lists BM5 in the same five-route Pennsylvania Avenue service set.

record_existence_and_kind.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select r.record_id,r.record_kind,r.display_name,rt.route_id,rt.route_type_normalized,json_extract(r.payload,'$.service_variant') as service_variant,r.primary_source_id from records r join routes rt on rt.record_id=r.record_id where r.record_id in (<selected aliases and targets>) order by rt.route_id,r.record_id;"
record_existence_and_kind.result: Returned all 11 selected alias/target records as record_kind=route. BM5 records have route_id BM5, route_type_normalized express, service_variant express. B82 Limited records have route_id B82, route_type_normalized limited_stop, service_variant limited_stop. Bx41 Local records have route_id Bx41, route_type_normalized local, service_variant local.

existing_alias_conflicts.identity_aliases_command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select kind,alias,target from identity_aliases where kind='route' and alias in (<selected aliases and targets>);"
existing_alias_conflicts.merges_json_command: jq -r --argjson ids '[<selected aliases and targets>]' '.aliases.route | to_entries[] | select(.key as $k | ($ids | index($k))) | [.key,.value] | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/merges.json

do_not_merge_conflicts.db_command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select kind,record_id_a,record_id_b,reason from do_not_merge where kind='route' and (record_id_a in (<selected aliases and targets>) or record_id_b in (<selected aliases and targets>));"
do_not_merge_conflicts.json_command: jq -r --argjson ids '[<selected aliases and targets>]' '.pairs.route[] | select(any(.record_ids[]; . as $rid | ($ids | index($rid)))) | [.record_ids[0],.record_ids[1],.reason] | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json

prior_prep_conflicts.command: jq -r --argjson ids '[<selected aliases>]' '.proposed_aliases[]? | select((.kind=="route" or .kind=="corridor") and ($ids | index(.alias_id))) | [.kind,.alias_id,.target_id] | @tsv' data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch0{47..96}-prep.json 2>/dev/null | sort -u
prior_prep_conflicts.result: Returned no rows; none of the selected alias_ids appeared in prior Codex identity prep artifacts through identity096.

scope_and_variant_checks.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select rt.route_id, rt.route_type_normalized, coalesce(json_extract(r.payload,'$.service_variant'),''), count(*) n, group_concat(r.record_id,' | ') ids from records r join routes rt on rt.record_id=r.record_id where r.record_kind='route' and rt.route_id is not null group by rt.route_id, rt.route_type_normalized, coalesce(json_extract(r.payload,'$.service_variant'),'') having n>1 order by n desc, rt.route_id limit 80;"
scope_and_variant_checks.result: Selected only route clusters with identical route_id and compatible normalized service variant/type: BM5 express, B82 limited_stop, and Bx41 local. No corridors were proposed, so no corridor-limit ambiguity is introduced.

source_evidence_commands.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select source_id,block_id,substr(replace(replace(raw_text,char(10),' '),char(13),' '),1,260) as quote from blocks_fts where (<selected evidence refs>) order by source_id,block_id;"
source_evidence_commands.result: Confirmed BM5 and B82 Limited source blocks explicitly mention BM5 and B82 Limited; confirmed Bx41 Local source blocks explicitly distinguish Bx41 Local from Bx41 LTD/SBS context.

source_evidence_commands.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "select e.record_id,e.source_id,e.block_id,e.page_number,json_extract(e.ref_json,'$.source_quote') as source_quote from evidence_refs e where e.record_id in (<selected aliases and targets>) order by e.record_id,e.ordinal;"
source_evidence_commands.result: Confirmed citeable evidence refs exist for each selected alias and target record.
